name: Rhode Island Data Transparency and Privacy Protection Act
summary: The Rhode Island Data Transparency and Privacy Protection Act establishes consumer data privacy rights for Rhode Island residents. It applies to entities conducting business in Rhode Island or targeting its residents that control or process personal data of at least 35,000 consumers, or 10,000 consumers if deriving more than 20% of revenue from selling personal data.
keyRequirements: Right to confirm whether a controller is processing personal data and to access that data
Right to correct inaccuracies in personal data
Right to delete personal data provided by or obtained about the consumer
Right to obtain a portable copy of personal data in a usable format
Right to opt out of sale of personal data, targeted advertising, and profiling
Consent required before processing sensitive data including biometric and health data
Controllers must maintain reasonable data security practices and provide transparent privacy notices
penalties: Enforced by the Rhode Island Attorney General. Civil penalties up to $10,000 per violation. No private right of action. Violators may also be subject to injunctive relief.
